en: The structural condition of the building and adjacent structures must be assessed by a qualified expert before demolition begins.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 110(1), 111(1), 118(6)
Hazardous, flammable, or explosive substances must be removed before demolition begins.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 110(2, 5, 7)
Utility locations and related safety measures must be identified before demolition begins.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 110(5)(5), 111(2), 112(5)
Debris accumulation must be controlled.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 111(5), 112(4), 114(2)(2)

During demolition work, the behavior of the structure being demolished must be monitored. Before dismantling wooden or metal structures, their connections must be inspected by the supervisor, and the structural components must be supported during dismantling when necessary. Structural stability must be re-examined after weather, vibration, or interruptions.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section , 111(1, 7), 114(3), 115(3)

Only the necessary machinery operators must remain in the area and others must stay outside the danger zone. Individuals must remain outside the danger zone during grabbing operations.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 115(4), 117(6), 118(7)

Fit and competent demolition operators must demonstrate physical, mental, and professional suitability for the tasks at hand. Suitability must be aligned with safe equipment operation.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 5(4)
